Research Areas

Our lab focuses on the intersection of thermodynamics, fluid mechanics, and materials science to develop sustainable energy solutions.

Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD)

We utilize advanced high-performance computing to simulate turbulent flows in complex geometries. Our work focuses on reducing drag in transportation systems and improving mixing efficiency in chemical reactors.

Key Projects:

  • Large Eddy Simulation of boundary layers
  • Optimization of airfoil designs
  • Multi-phase flow modeling

Sustainable Thermal Systems

Addressing the global energy challenge requires efficient thermal management. We research phase-change materials and novel heat exchanger surfaces to maximize energy recovery in industrial processes.

Key Projects:

  • Waste heat recovery in manufacturing
  • Thermal battery development
  • Solar-thermal energy storage
